behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the affected directory and spec file; tests check that file's contents, which is cited.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the bug symptom and general cause (skipping ID regeneration for nested array items) but doesn't state the literal fix.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_skip_condition_uses_exact_match asserts 'clipboardPath ===' appears in lines 117-123 and that '.endsWith(\'.id\')' is absent; an equivalent correct fix using e.g. a different comparison (like checking path depth, using !== with negation, or refactoring to a helper function) would fail these text-matching assertions.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The primary f2p tests (test_skip_condition_uses_exact_match, test_no_endsWith_in_skip_logic) are pure text/grep checks on source lines 117-123/119; only test_all_existing_tests_pass runs code but it passes on both base and fixed per the docstring.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests only grep for the literal string 'clipboardPath ===' in a line range; they don't verify the merge behavior, so a stub/no-op fix that merely edits the source text would pass without fixing actual paste behavior.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction promises that 'all 8 existing tests should continue to pass' (behavioral), but the primary new f2p assertions are textual; the UI duplication scenario described isn't tested behaviorally beyond reusing existing unit tests which pass on base too.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings explaining fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass intent.

no_typos.reason: Paths and filenames appear consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to the directory but does not cite exact line numbers or the exact buggy expression.
